original: No node has epistemic authority over truth claims.
mutant: [INVERTED] The causal arrow in PHIL-13 points the wrong way. The effect is actually the cause.
test: Check temporal ordering: did the supposed cause in PHIL-13 actually precede the supposed effect in git history?
evidence: Arrow IS partially inverted. 0/75 human signals rejected across 520 sessions (L-994). Actual flow: human signal→immediate implementation→post-hoc evidence marshaling. PHIL-13 claim created by human directive, persists via human deference. Evidence: HQ-16 thru HQ-35 batch-closed 19 items without evaluation. L-341 documents asymmetry. SIG-46/SIG-48 created PHIL-21/PHIL-22 same-session without intermediate testing. Formal evidence infrastructure exists (L-341 challenge-acceptance path) but directional authority is de facto epistemic authority.
